faqs.q: What is the difference between an internal and external locus of control?
faqs.a: With an internal locus of control, you tend to feel that your own choices and effort drive what happens. With an external locus of control, you tend to feel that outcomes come from luck, fate, or other people.

faqs.q: Is locus of control the same as confidence or optimism?
faqs.a: Not quite. Locus of control is specifically about who or what you believe causes outcomes, while confidence and optimism are separate qualities, even though they can overlap.

Yes, these beliefs build up over a lifetime of experiences and can shift. Therapy frequently works to adjust an overly external view by helping someone notice the choices and actions that are genuinely theirs to make.
